roni.label: RONI
roni.description: ENSO index with tropical-mean SST anomaly removed — isolates the ENSO signal from global warming. 3-month running mean anchored to central month.

oni.label: ONI
oni.description: SST anomaly in the Niño-3.4 region (5°N–5°S, 170°W–120°W), 3-month running mean anchored to central month. 1991–2020 base period.

pna.label: PNA
pna.description: Winter-dominant wave train over the Pacific–North American sector; positive phase = ridge over the west coast + trough over the eastern US.

nao.label: NAO
nao.description: North Atlantic pressure dipole (Icelandic low vs Azores high). Positive = strong westerlies, mild NW European winters.

ao.label: AO
ao.description: Hemispheric NH annular mode — leading EOF of 1000 hPa geopotential N of 20°N. Positive = strong polar vortex, cold air bottled up.

pdo.label: PDO
pdo.description: Leading EOF of monthly N-Pacific SST anomalies (N of 20°N). Decadal-scale: sustained warm or cool phases lasting 20-30 years that modulate ENSO teleconnections, US drought / fishery regimes. Mantua et al. (1997).

amm.label: AMM
amm.description: Cross-equatorial SST + trade-wind mode in the tropical Atlantic. Positive = warm N, cool S, ITCZ pulled north → stronger W-African monsoon and more-active Atlantic hurricane season. Chiang & Vimont (2004).

pmm.label: PMM
pmm.description: Subtropical-Pacific analogue of the AMM — extratropical stochastic forcing projects onto a cross-equatorial SST/trade-wind pattern that can seed ENSO through the seasonal footprinting mechanism. Chiang & Vimont (2004).

tni.label: TNI
tni.description: Standardized Niño-1+2 SSTa minus standardized Niño-4 SSTa — captures the east–central tropical Pacific SST gradient. Distinguishes Eastern-Pacific (EP) from Central-Pacific (CP, 'Modoki') ENSO events independent of overall warm/cold state. Trenberth & Stepaniak (2001).

npgo.label: NPGO
npgo.description: Second EOF of N-Pacific SSH (and SST) — the low-frequency gyre-circulation mode that pairs with the PDO. Tracks Kuroshio Extension shifts, California Current nutrient supply, and ecosystem regime change. Di Lorenzo et al. (2008).
